programmes.show_description: Workmen in Canada film an enormous and enraged humanoid figure appearing to throw a tree trunk in the forests of Alberta. (S4, ep 2)
Glacier National Park: In Montana's Glacier National Park, some claim that there is a hidden underground Nazi base that may even have been the final hideout for Hitler himself. (S1, ep 5)
Mount Shasta National Forest: From inexplicable disappearances to mysterious experiments, events in Mount Shasta National Park may be explained by an ancient Karuk Indian legend. (S1, ep 6)
Atmospheric Anomalies: Some experts believe many atmospheric light shows are actually caused by extraterrestrials. If so, do they pose an imminent threat to humanity? (S3, ep 4)
Alien Apocalypse: If extraterrestrials visited Earth would they enlighten us or destroy us? Experts examine the possibility of a doomsday orchestrated by an alien enemy. (S3, ep 5)
Top Ten Military Encounters: A fascinating and shocking countdown of the top ten military encounters between Earth's armed forces and UFOs. (S3, ep 6)
Alien Skies: From mid-air abductions to near-disasters caused by unseen enemies, experts examine if we are alone in the skies over Earth. (S3 ep 7)
The Next Wave: A fascinating look at the mounting evidence that suggests UFO waves are able to penetrate the world's most closely guarded airspace at will. (S3, ep 8)
Signs of Abduction: From loss of time and bizarre hazy memories, to terrifying accounts of alien surgery, these are the hidden signs of alien abduction. (S3, ep 9)
Antigravity: Experts discuss the possibility of advanced alien technology that could allow UFOs to fly at blinding speed or hover effortlessly a few feet above the ground. (S3, ep 10)
UFO Metal: Nations all over the world are rumoured to have found metal debris from UFO crashes. Join the investigation into the alleged wreckages of otherworldly crafts. (S3, ep 11)
Attack of the Semi: Tempers flare when a dump truck driver clashes with an angry motorist over an exhaust, turning a minor dispute into a heated standoff. (S1, ep 11)
Highway Robbery: Road rage escalates as a Pennsylvania taxi driver faces a gunman and a Texas motorist threatens others with an axe. (S1, ep 12)
Drama Online Part 2: Ashley has to face the consequences of her rash decision, Seth takes a ride in a hovercraft and Les does business with Muhammad Ali Jr. (S11, ep 6)
Daddy Daughter Dance: When Ashley steals a deal from right under Les's nose, the family decides to hold a father-daughter sales competition. (S11, ep 7)
Seth's Gamble Part 1: When Les finds out that Seth has spent thousands of dollars at a sports card auction, he challenges his son to sell all the new items within a week. (S11, ep 8)
Seth's Gamble Part 2: After spending a fortune on sports memorabilia, Seth rushes to sell it all before his dad's deadline. Later, Les checks out a Starfire Convertible. (S11, ep 9)
Seth's Soft Side: Seth has his eye on a valuable guitar, a customer tries to buy a fancy watch and Les strikes a deal. (S11, ep 10)
Guards Go Home: When the security guards start spooking customers, Les sends them home to teach them a lesson. (S11, ep 11)
Episode 9: Henry and Skid score big in Derbyshire, revisit Ed for a missed bike and tackle repairs before Skid and Allen brave the sales stand alone. (S4, ep 9)
Toyota Sera: The guys find a Toyota Sera - a rare concept car with gull-wing doors and a 50s-style glass roof - but it's not driveable in the way they need it. (S3, ep 10)
Secrets of Ancient Structures: Age-old ruins can stand with their true history seemingly erased by time - can modern research discover why these ancient structures were built? (S4, ep 2)
Mysteries of the Vikings: The Vikings were infamous for being bloodthirsty, seafaring warriors - but historians are learning surprising new things about this Norse society. (S4, ep 3)
Strange Stories of the Presidents: Join William Shatner as he explores the bizarre tales involving US presidents that defy explanation and are still shrouded in mystery. (S4, ep 4)
Unbreakable Codes: For centuries, mankind has devised ingenious ways to hide information, from enigmatic puzzles to secret symbols. Can we unlock these great mysteries? (S4, ep 7)
Strange Tales of WWII: Throughout history's largest and deadliest human conflict, a number of intriguing and persistent mysteries were born - from lost gold to secret societies. (S4, ep 8)
Secrets of the Apostles: Ancient texts shed new light on the many competing Christian sects that sprang up in the first centuries after Christ's crucifixion. (Part 1 of 2)

programmes.show_name: The Mystery Of The Holy Lance
programmes.show_description: Explore the fascinating 2,000-year-long history of the Holy Lance, a sacred relic of Christianity sought by Emperor Constantine, Napoleon Bonaparte and Adolf Hitler.

programmes.show_name: Book Of Secrets With Lance Reddick
programmes.show_description: The Hunt for Hitler's Relics: Adolf Hitler was deeply into the occult - so much so that he even established a Nazi organisation with a secret mission to confiscate relics. (S4, ep 5)
Chip off the Orb Block: Notorious phenomenon-experiencer Chris Bledsoe's daughter Emily may share his abilities and they face the Monroe Institute and the Everglades. (S3, ep 4)
Bursting the Bubble: As the team launches high-powered rockets into the sky on Skinwalker Ranch, they are frozen in shock by what they encounter. (S6, ep 4)
The Sunken Legend: A powerful hurricane forces Jeff's team into a secluded cove, where they investigate The Lombrac, an 18th-century ghost ship. (S1, ep 7)
Unfinished Business: The team must make one final push to find the Chameau's remaining fortune and complete the mission Jeff's father started decades ago before time runs out. (S1, ep 8)
A mysterious entity is spotted in the Irish countryside. A séance goes violently wrong and a UFO pays an overnight visit to the city that never sleeps. (S8, ep 1)
Children across North America interact with ghosts that their parents can't see and citizens in China fear the worst as hundreds of orbs swarm in the moonlight. (S8, ep 2)
UFO Over the Andes, the Ohio Grassman, and Ancient Horn Mystery: Did a Chilean family photograph a real UFO over the Andes? And does a huge fossil point to a prehistoric figure? (S4, ep 2)
Men in Black Calls, Old Kentucky Aliens, and Mysterious Eclipse: What is the identity of a bizarre creature spotted on a driveway? And did the Men in Black threaten a man? (S4, ep 3)
Falcon 9 Mystery, Demonic Wall Climber and Leningrad Alien Fetus: Are reports of a strange swirling vortex with a brilliant white center in the skies proof of a genuine UFO? (S4, ep 9)
Elizabethan Plymouth: Alice explores the stories of England's 'Golden Age', from Sir Francis Drake to the Spanish Armada, piracy, theft, and the slave trade. (S3, ep 4)
Portsmouth Age Of Empire: In Portsmouth, Alice Roberts learns about Nelson's dark side, the heat of the battle at Trafalgar, the role of the Navy in the British Empire, and more. (S3, ep 3)
Wolverhampton 2: The dealers come across a music box, and some letters from former prime ministers Edward Heath and Harold Macmillan land on Stewart Hogfather's table. (S10, ep 25)
Darting and Dodging: Rick and Corey dart out of the shop to check out a 1968 convertible. Meanwhile, Chum desperately wants a piece of an oreodont fossil. (S24, ep 15)
...Henry's Wreck-trospective. The Fairford Wreck-Trospective: Dive headfirst into the archives to celebrate the very best bits from the hit series Junk & Disorderly. (S1, ep 6)
A Ricky Runs Through It: Victor battles to end his losing streak in Waxahachie, where some buyers prove that playing it tough is the only way to make a killing. (S3, ep 3)
Mayor Of Money Town: Victor gets more than he bargained for when he pursues a motorcycle in Carrollton. Ricky finds himself face to face with one of his biggest fears. (S4, ep 5)
This Can't Be: Brandon and Lori take a risk on a large covered item during an unusual auction. Will the reveal rev their profits or stall their cash flow? (S2, ep 10)
Swollen Profits: A locker unleashes a swarm of more than just merchandise while Jesse throws down to win antiques. (S2, ep 11)
Workmen in Canada film an enormous and enraged humanoid figure appearing to throw a tree trunk in the forests of Alberta. (S4, ep 2)
Residents of a village reported seeing a statue of the Virgin Mary move, but some believe the phenomenon was just an optical illusion or a prank. (S1, ep 5)
Millions mourned the death of Elvis Presley in 1977, but he's been spotted hundreds of times since. (S1, ep 8)
People reveal details from their past lives as celebrities, while experts investigate evidence that suggests death may not be the end of our existence. (S1, ep 9)
In 1977, schoolchildren spot a cigar-shaped aircraft in Wales. The sighting transforms the quiet seaside town into a UFO hot spot. (S1, ep 11)
Alleged sightings of leprechauns and fairies are nothing new in Ireland. Experts debate whether these Celtic creatures are fact or fiction. (S1, ep 13)
Experts investigate why religious apparitions appear on seemingly normal objects to seemingly normal people. Is it blind faith or supernatural phenomena? (S1, ep 17)
Believers and skeptics debate the origins and meanings of the mysterious formations known as crop circles. (S1, ep 20)
Experts investigate if a series of house fires across the UK are linked to an allegedly cursed portrait. (S1, ep 22)
A strange creature was reportedly seen in a wooded area of Massachusetts by different people, all giving similar descriptions. Coincidence, collusion, or a genuine mystery? (S1, ep 24)
What is the Voynich Manuscript, and what secrets does the mysterious 500-year-old document hold? (S1, ep 26)
Antiquities expert Timothy Smith looks into the existence of a code hidden in the world's oldest complete text of the Hebrew Bible, which some believe to be the actual word of God.
This Can't Be: Brandon and Lori take a risk on a large covered item during an unusual auction. Will the reveal rev their profits or stall their cash flow? (S2, ep 10)
Swollen Profits: A locker unleashes a swarm of more than just merchandise while Jesse throws down to win antiques. (S2, ep 11)
A Ricky Runs Through It: Victor battles to end his losing streak in Waxahachie, where some buyers prove that playing it tough is the only way to make a killing. (S3, ep 3)
Mayor Of Money Town: Victor gets more than he bargained for when he pursues a motorcycle in Carrollton. Ricky finds himself face to face with one of his biggest fears. (S4, ep 5)
Wolverhampton 3: Items of interest include a book about the Titanic which arrives on David Tupman's table, while Karen gets a little too carried away with a cavalry sword. (S10, ep 26)
Pushing Boundaries: When the team deploys a cutting-edge new technology on Skinwalker Ranch to study the mysterious Bubble, it leads to an unsettling revelation. (S6, ep 5)
Purgatory Bridge: Andy and Paul return to the Ranch, at the bridge that has been dubbed The Miller Triangle and find more evidence that the two sites are linked. (S3, ep 5)
Building a Super Power: Witness the truth behind the Ancient Egyptian superpower we know today as experts dive into the history of their iconic monuments and strong leaders. (S2, ep 1)
